title: 10 medicinal constituents of Elaeagnus umbellata (oleaster)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Elaeagnus umbellata oleocanthal is a type of flavonoid extracted from the seeds of the Chinese silverberry (Elaeagnus umbellata) plant. It has been gaining attention for its potential health benefits, particularly in relation to cardiovascular disease and cancer prevention. Studies have shown that oleocanthal exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Its unique chemical composition also suggests it could be a valuable addition to functional foods and beverages.

Elaeagnus umbellata quercetin is a flavonoid glycoside extracted from the seeds of this plant. It has been found to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Quercetin has been shown to inhibit the proliferation of cancer cells and induce apoptosis, making it a potential candidate for cancer therapy. Its antioxidant activity may also help protect against oxidative stress-related diseases.

Elaeagnus umbellata kaempferol is a flavonoid compound that has been isolated from the fruit and leaves of this plant. It exhibits antioxidant properties and has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory activity, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases such as cardiovascular disorders, cancer, and neurodegenerative diseases. Kaempferol has also been found to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities, contributing to its traditional uses in folk medicine for treating various infections.

Elaeagnus umbellata isorhapontigenin is a bioactive compound isolated from the fruit of Elaeagnus umbellata, a species of flowering plant native to Asia. It has been found to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Isorhapontigenin has also shown promise in treating cardiovascular disease by inhibiting platelet aggregation and reducing blood lipid levels. Its potential as a therapeutic agent is currently being researched further.

Elaeagnus umbellata ursolic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the fruit of the plant. It has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Ursolic acid has been shown to exhibit anticancer activity by inhibiting tumor cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis. Additionally, it may also help to improve glucose metabolism and reduce oxidative stress in cells. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of ursolic acid on human health.

Elaeagnus umbellata triterpenoids is a type of bioactive compound isolated from its fruit. These compounds have been found to possess various pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Additionally, they have been reported to exhibit cytotoxicity against certain cancer cell lines, making them a potential lead for the development of novel anticancer agents.

Elaeagnus umbellata flavonoids is a group of plant compounds that have been identified in its leaves and stems. These flavonoids are responsible for the yellow to orange coloration of the plant's flowers, fruits, and seeds. They have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to the plant's traditional uses in herbal medicine.

Elaeagnus umbellata phenolic acids is a group of bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds are present in the leaves, stems, and seeds of the plant and exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The most prominent phenolic acids found in Elaeagnus umbellata include rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and quinic acid, which have been shown to possess cytotoxic effects on various cancer cell lines, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of certain diseases.

Elaeagnus umbellata ellagic acid is a bioactive compound isolated from its fruit, leaves, and stems. It has been extensively studied for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Ellagic acid has been shown to exhibit anticancer activity by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, while also demonstrating potential in preventing cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and neurodegenerative disorders due to its ability to scavenge free radicals and modulate various cellular pathways.

Elaeagnus umbellata gallic acid is a type of phenolic compound found in the fruit and leaves of this plant. It has been studied for its potential bioactive properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Gallic acid is believed to contribute to the plant's natural defense mechanisms against pathogens and pests, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and diseases in humans.

intro: Elaeagnus umbellata has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These medicinal properties enable it to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities, making it effective in treating various health issues. For instance, its flavonoid content may help alleviate symptoms of allergic rhinitis and improve respiratory functions, while its antibacterial properties can aid in wound healing and infection prevention.
intro_desc: Elaeagnus umbellata has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds exhibit medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids in oleaster may help to protect against heart disease and certain cancers, while the saponins can aid in reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Regular consumption of oleaster may also improve cardiovascular health and boost the immune system, contributing to an overall healthier and longer life.
